info.title: Wincher API
info.description:
The Wincher Public API.

# Introduction

Our API is accessed over HTTPS, uses standard HTTP methods and verbs and is
(mostly) built around RESTful principles. The root endpoint is
`https://api.wincher.com/v1` and all data is sent and received as
JSON.

# Authentication

You authenticate against the API by using a Personal Access Token. You can
generate a key by logging into your account and navigating to "[Personal
Access Tokens](/account/personal-access-tokens)" in your user settings.
Generated tokens can also be revoked from there. The token must be supplied
via the `Authorization` header as a `Bearer` token.

# Rate Limiting

The API may limit requests made to the API. We currently allow 5000 requests
per hour. If your request is rate limited the API will respond with a `429`
status code.

# Versioning

The API version is suffixed to the root endpoint. For example, the current
version is `v1` and is accessible at `https://api.wincher.com/v1`.

## Backwards compatible changes

We consider the following changes backwards compatible and should be kept in
mind when building API clients:

* Adding new API endpoints and resources.
* Adding optional parameters to existing endpoints/resources.
* Adding new properties to API responses.
* Changing the order of properties in API responses.
* Changing the length of identifiers and the like.

Regardless, these changes are always documented in the change log.

## Change Log
* 2025-09-18
  - ���uDFE1 **[important]**: Position tracking is now limited to the top 30 positions instead of 100. This affects the `ranking.position` property in operations like [`List keywords`](#operation/listKeywords). You can [read more about this](https://www.wincher.com/blog/serp-tracking-depth-limited) change in our official blog.
  - Position distributions 31-100 and 101-101 are no longer returned and are not in use anymore. A new position distribution 31-31 has been introduced to replace them. This affects the `position_distribution` array in the `RankingSummary` model used by operations like [`List competitor ranking summaries`](#operation/listCompetitorRankingSummaries).
  - Existing segments including the position value filters will be updated to reflect the new position tracking depth.
* 2025-06-27
  - ���uDFE1 **[deprecate]**: The `Volume.forecast` property is deprecated. From now on it will always be an empty array.
* 2025-01-11
  - ���uDFE1 **[deprecate]**: The properties `tracked_website_ids` and `competitor_to_website_ids` on `SERP.results` are now deprecated and will always be empty. Use `Website.domain` and `Website.competitors` instead.
* 2025-01-10
  - Added `seo_score` to `SERPResult` for `listSerps` endpoint.
* 2024-12-16
  - ���uDD34 **[breaking]**: The `Quota.limit` and `Quota.remaining` properties are now nullable. A `null` value implies an unlimited quota.
* 2024-12-19
   - Added support for `FOUND_ON_WEB` SERP feature.
* 2024-11-27
   - Added `competitors` to `Website`. This can replace usages of the `listCompetitors` endpoint. 
* 2024-11-19
   - Added support for `LOCAL_PACK` SERP feature.
* 2024-09-25
  - Add optional `include_serp_features` (default: `true`) query parameter to `getWebsite`, `listWebsites`, `getKeywordGroup`, `listKeywordGroups` and `listCompetitorRankingSummaries`. Setting this to `false` can substantially reduce response time and size when calling these endpoints.
* 2024-09-05
  - Add constraints to `KeywordGroup.name` for min and max string length.
* 2024-08-21
   - Rename `map_tracking_units` in `Account.limits` to `map_tracking_credits`.
   - Add new plan feature `map_tracking_credits`.
* 2024-08-14
   - Added support for `AI_OVERVIEW` SERP feature.
* 2024-06-25
   - Added `start_date` to `UpdateWebsite`.
   - Added `start_date` to `Website`.
* 2024-06-10
   - Added support for `DISCUSSIONS_AND_FORUMS` SERP feature.
* 2024-05-09
   - Added new quotas to Account limits
* 2024-05-01
   - Added `maxLength: 45` to `Website.display_name` property. Note that this was already the allowed maximum length for the given value that our system would truncate to if the string length was exceeded over 45 characters.
* 2024-04-24
   - Added `maxLength: 45` to `Project.name` property. Note that this was already the allowed maximum length for the given value that our system would truncate to if the string length was exceeded over 45 characters.
* 2024-04-19
   - Added `map_tracking_units` to `Account.limits`
* 2024-03-18
   - Added `forecast` to `Volume`.
* 2024-02-16
   - Added `keyword_lookups` to `Account.limits`.
* 2023-11-08
   - Added `Keyword.ranking.best_position` that contains the best position ever for the keyword.
* 2023-10-03
   - Added `"NOT IN"` segment operator support. Currently supported only for `group` field filter type.
* 2023-07-20
  - Added `brand_keywords` property of strings array type to the `Website` and `UpdateWebsite` object schema.
    This value will be obtainable and modifiable with the [`Websites` related APIs](#tag/Websites).
  - Added `is_branded` segment filter for including only those keywords which are tracked as brand keywords.
* 2023-07-17
   - Added `users` limit and usage to `Account`.
* 2023-07-14
  - Added `groups_count` segment filter for filtering keywords by the count of assigned groups.
* 2023-07-05
  - Added `change_status` to `Keyword.position`.
* 2023-06-22
  - Added `pages_count` segment filter for filtering the amount of ranking pages.
* 2023-03-22
  - ���uDFE1 **[deprecate]** CPC `value` properties `cpc.value` and `cpc.history[0].value` are deprecated for keyword response models in operations: [`listKeywords`](#operation/listKeywords), [`createKeyword`](#operation/createKeyword), [`getKeyword`](#operation/getKeyword), [`updateKeyword`](#operation/updateKeyword), [`createKeywordsBulk`](#operation/createKeywordsBulk) and [`updateKeywordsBulk`](#operation/updateKeywordsBulk).
    - There is a new sibling property `high_value` which should be favored and will present the same value as `value` property.
    - The `value` properties will stop working after 2023-12-31.
  - New optional query parameter `include_ranking=true` (default: `true`) for operation [`getKeyword`](#operation/getKeyword) which if not provided or is specified as `true` will ensure that a value is provided for the `ranking` property (which is the current default behavior for this endpoint).
  - Adds `data.volume` and `data.results[].traffic` to response model of operation [`listSerps`](#operation/listSerps).
* 2023-01-10
  - Added optional query params `segment_start_at` and `segment_end_at` to endpoints using `segment_id` in order to specify which date to be used in segment filtering. They will default to `start_at` and `end_at`.
* 2022-12-16
  - Added `share_of_voice` and `volume` to `RankingSummary` and related APIs.
* 2022-11-11
  - Added `preferred_url` to `Keyword` and related APIs.
* 2022-10-19
  - Added `owned_serp_feature` filter type.
* 2022-09-22
  - Added keyword count history to `Website`.
* 2022-09-22
  - Added ranking pages count history to `RankingSummary`.
* 2022-09-22
  - Added SERP feature count history to `RankingSummary`.
* 2022-09-22
  - Added traffic value to `RankingSummary`.
* 2022-09-22
  - Added competition level to `Keyword`.
* 2022-09-16
  - Added two new competitor ranking data endpoints: [`GET /websites/{website_id}/competitors/ranking-summaries`](#operation/listCompetitorRankingSummaries) and [`GET /websites/{website_id}/competitors/keyword-positions`](#operation/listCompetitorKeywordPositions).
* 2022-06-19
  - ���uDFE1 **[deprecate]**: `POST /websites/{website_id}/ranking-summary`, will stop working after 2022-12-31.
* 2022-06-19
  - Add pagination support via `limit` and `offset` parameters.
* 2022-06-19
  - Add Segments endpoints and support filtering of data via the `segment_id` parameter.
* 2022-04-19
  - ���uDFE1 **[deprecate]**: `POST /websites/{website_id}/competitors/rankings`, will stop working after 2022-12-31.
* 2022-02-01
  - ���uDD34 **[breaking]**: Added `display_name` property to `Website` and `UpdateWebsite`.
* 2021-11-02
  - ���uDD34 **[breaking]**: Removed `type` from `Account`.
* 2021-09-14
  - Added `ranking_updated_at` property to `Keyword`.
* 2021-08-04
  - ���uDD34 **[breaking]**: `position`, `traffic`, `volume` and `cpc` objects can now be null if there is no data and their subproperties are now not nullable.

# Data types

## Formats

* Countries are always `ISO-3166-1 alpha-2` codes, for example `SE`.
* Languages are always `ISO-639-1` codes, for example `sv`.
* Currency is always `ISO-4217` codes, for example: `EUR`.
* Dates are always formatted according to `ISO-8601`, for example `2021-05-09`.
* Date-times are always formatted according to `ISO-8601` with UTC as the time zone, for example `2021-05-09T00:00:00Z`.

## Collections
Collections are *always* returned as an object with a `data` field
containing the items. This also holds true for bulk creates/updates.

tags.name: User
tags.description: Endpoint to get information about the currently authenticated user.

tags.name: Account
tags.description: Endpoint to get account information for the currently authenticated user.

tags.name: Search Engines
tags.description: Endpoint to get search engines used as input by various other endpoints.

tags.name: Locations
tags.description: Endpoint to get locations used as input by various other endpoints.

tags.name: Projects
tags.description: Endpoints to manage the account‘s projects and get their ranking data.

tags.name: Websites
tags.description: Endpoints to manage the account‘s websites and get summarized ranking data for the entire website.

tags.name: Keywords
tags.description: Endpoints to manage websites‘ keywords and get their ranking data.

tags.name: Keyword Groups
tags.description: Endpoints to manage websites‘ keyword groups and get their ranking data.

tags.name: SERP
tags.description: Endpoint to get parsed search engine result pages for keywords.

tags.name: Competitors
tags.description: Endpoints to manage websites‘ competitors and get their ranking data.

tags.name: Segments
tags.description: Endpoints to manage the account‘s segments.

value.description: Represents the high value. Should not be used due to deprecation and instead it should be replaced with the sibling property `high_value`.

domain.description: Domain name of the search engine.

languages.description: Available language codes for the search engine.

created_at.description: When the account was first created.

limit.description: The maximum number of websites that can be added to this account (null equals unlimited).

usage.description: The current number of websites on the account.

limit.description: The maximum number of users that can be added to this account (null equals unlimited).

usage.description: The current number of users on the account.

limit.description: The maximum number of keywords that can be added to this account (null equals unlimited).

usage.description: The current number of keywords on the account.

map_tracking_credits.description: Map tracking credits limit and usage values.

limit.description: The maximum number of local map tracking SERP check credits that can be added to this account.

usage.description: The current number of SERP check credits.

history_days.description: The number of days of history this account can view.

Quota.description: A service usage quota including the limit, the current amount of usage, the remaining amount, the quota interval e.g. daily or monthly, and the quota reset date. The `null` value implies an unlimited quota.

usage.description: This is the already used amount.

remaining.description: This is the available and remaining amount.

resets_at.description: This is the quota reset date i.e. the whole limit amount will be available again.

language.description: The preferred language code (ISO 639-1) for the user.

project_ids.description: The projects this user has access to.

domain.description: Website domain name, including subdomain. Domains without a subdomain act as a wildcard when collecting rankings.

display_name.description: Website display name. If set to an empty string the `domain` will be used instead.

keyword_count.description: The number of keywords this website has.

keyword_count_history.description: The history for number of tracked keywords during the period.

keyword_group_count.description: The number of keyword groups this website has.

competitor_count.description: The number of competitors this website has.

brand_keywords.description: A strings only array of unique keywords important for the brand related to this website.

start_date.description: The date SEO work began for the website. Is used when selecting the "Since start" date period in reports.

display_name.description: Website display name. If set to an empty string the `domain` will be used instead.

brand_keywords.description: A strings only array of unique keywords important for the brand related to this website.

start_date.description: The date SEO work began for the website. Is used when selecting the "Since start" date period in reports.

value.description: Last known monthly search volume for the keyword.

Value.description: The latest value in the period.

DownscaledHistory.description:
Historical data. This data is downscaled depending on the
length of the chosen period and may not contain every
available data point.

Position.description: The best position in the SERP.

change_status.description:
The status of this keyword's ranking change during the period.

| Status    | Description                                                                                 |
|-----------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| UNCHANGED | The keyword has not changed its ranking during the period.                                  |
| IMPROVED  | The keyword has improved its ranking during the period.                                     |
| DECLINED  | The keyword has declined its ranking during the period.                                     |
| NEW       | The keyword was not ranking in the beginning of the period but is ranking at the end of it. |
| LOST      | The keyword was ranking in the beginning of the period but is not ranking at the end of it. |

Traffic.description: The estimated number of clicks to the website based on the position.

ranking.description:
The latest ranking data during the period. The period is specified
by using the `start_at` and `end_at` parameters. Will be `null` if
the ranking has never been updated for the keyword or if
`include_ranking` is not set to `true`.

pages.description:
The pages that this website is ranking with for, sorted by the
position (best first).

serp_features.description: The features included in the latest SERP for the keyword.

volume.description: The search volume for the keyword at the time of the SERP.

is_owned.description: Whether the feature is owned by the tracked website.

traffic.description: The estimated number of clicks to the website based on the best position.

RankingSummary.description: Aggregated ranking data for the filtered keywords.

value.description: The average position in the SERP.

change.description: The change of the average position in the period.

history.description: The average position history during the period.

value.description: The estimated number of clicks to the website based on the position.

change.description: The change in traffic during the period.

history.description: The traffic history during the period.

value.description: The estimated value of clicks to the website based on the position.

change.description: The change in traffic value during the period.

history.description: The traffic value history during the period.

change.description: The change in total keyword volume.

history.description: The total volume history during the period.

change.description: The change in share of voice.

history.description: The share of voice history during the period.

value.description: The total number of positions gained during the period.

history.description: The history of positions gained during the period.

value.description: The total number of positions lost during the period.

history.description: The history of positions lost during the period.

range_start.description: The start of the position range.

range_end.description: The end of the position range.

history.description: The position distribution history during the period.

value.description: The number of keywords with this SERP feature.

change.description: The change in the number of keywords with this SERP feature during the period.

history.description: The history of number of keywords with this SERP feature during the period.

change.description: The change in the number of ranking pages during the period.

history.description: The history of the number of ranking pages during the period.

type.description: The type of annotation. When creating an annotation `type` must be `USER`.

date.description: The date this annotation should be shown at.

description.description: Description for the annotation. This is displayed in the graphs.

keyword_ids.description: Keywords this annotation is related to. If null it's treated as a global annotation.

value.description: Last known competition level for the keyword.
Last known high CPC (cost per click) value for the keyword. Should not be used due to deprecation and instead should be replaced by using the sibling property `high_value`.

high_value.description: Last known high CPC (cost per click) value for the keyword.

low_value.description: Last known low CPC (cost per click) value for the keyword.

operator.description:
| Operator | Description                                              |
|----------|----------------------------------------------------------|
| =        | Equals                                                   |
| !=       | Does not equal                                           |
| <        | Less than                                                |
| >        | Greater than                                             |
| <=       | Less than or equals                                      |
| >=       | Greater than or equals                                   |
| ~        | Contains                                                 |
| !~       | Does not contain                                         |
| ^        | Starts with                                              |
| !^       | Does not start with                                      |
| $        | Ends with                                                |
| !$       | Does not end with                                        |
| IN       | Is one of (value must be an array of strings or numbers) |
| NOT IN   | Is none of (value must be an array of strings)           |

name.description: Name of the segment. If no name is given the segment is considered temporary.

IncludeRanking.name: include_ranking
IncludeRanking.description: If true, will include ranking data in the response. Setting this to `true` substantially increases the response time and size.

IncludeRankingDefaultTrue.name: include_ranking
IncludeRankingDefaultTrue.description: If true, will include ranking data in the response. Setting this to `false` substantially reduces the response time and size.

IncludeKeywordCountHistory.name: include_keyword_count_history
IncludeKeywordCountHistory.description: If true, will include `keyword_count_history` in the response.

IncludeSerpFeatures.name: include_serp_features
IncludeSerpFeatures.description: If true, will include `serp_features` in the response. Setting this to `false` substantially reduces the response time and size.

StartAt.name: start_at
StartAt.description: The start date and time for the data.

EndAt.name: end_at
EndAt.description: The end date and time for the data.

SegmentStartAt.name: segment_start_at
SegmentStartAt.description: The start date and time to check against for the segment filtering.

SegmentEndAt.name: segment_end_at
SegmentEndAt.description: The end date and time to check against for the segment filtering.

schema.description: The maximum number of results to return.
Only get results after this offset.
Segment to filter the keywords and ranking data by.
The field to order the results by. Prepend with `-` to use descending ordering.

get.description: Lists locations based on a search query or country.

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Free text search query (minimum 3 characters long).

parameters.name: country
parameters.description: Country code to filter the results by.

schema.description: Whether or not to only return named segments.

post.description:
Creates multiple keywords at once. Any invalid or already existing
keywords will be ignored and not be created. A maximum of 1000 keywords
can be created at once.

delete.description:
Deletes multiple keywords at once. Invalid keywords are silently
ignored. A maximum of 1000 keywords can be deleted at a time.

keyword_ids.description: Array of Keyword IDs to delete.

put.description:
Updates multiple keywords at once. Invalid keywords are silently
ignored. A maximum of 1000 keywords can be updated at a time.

schema.description: Array of Keyword objects to update.

get.description: Lists available SERPs for a keyword. Is limited to returning at most 30 SERPs at a time.
List summary data for all competitors and the website itself.
List keyword positions for all competitors and the website itself.

get.summary: Get historical rankings for a keyword
get.description: Get historical rankings for the website and keyword.

parameters.name: group_by
parameters.description: If set, will group the result into multiple series and otherwise return a single serie for the keyword.

post.description: Get historical rankings for the website and the given keywords.
